ahoy: interjection. Used to hail a ship, a boat or a person, or to attract attention.
any: adverb. To even the slightest extent, at all.
arroyo: noun. A dry creek or streambed, a gulch which temporarily or seasonally fills and flows (after sufficient rain).
hao: noun. A former currency unit of Vietnam, worth one tenth of a dong.
hay: noun. Grass cut and dried for use as animal fodder.
hoar: noun. A white or greyish - white colour.
hon: noun. A term of endearment; Honey, sweetheart
honor: noun. Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration.
honorary: adjective. Given as an honor, with no duties attached, and without payment.
hooray: interjection. Used to express approval, joy or victory.
hora: noun. A circle dance popular in the Balkans, Israel and Yiddish culture worldwide.
horary: adjective. Pertaining to an hour or hours.
horn: noun. A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals, usually paired.
horny: adjective. Hard or bony, like an animal's horn.
hoy: noun. A small coaster vessel, usually sloop-rigged, used in conveying passengers and goods, or as a tender to larger vessels in port.
hoya: noun. Any plant of the genus Hoya
noh: noun. A form of classical Japanese music drama.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
oar: noun. A type of lever used to propel a boat, having a flat blade at one end and a handle at the other, and pivoted in a rowlock atop the
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
orra: adjective. Superfluous; odd, unmatched, left over.
rah: interjection. An exclamation of support or encouragement.
ray: noun. A beam of light or radiation.
rayon: noun. A manufactured regenerated cellulosic fiber.
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
roan: adjective. Having a coat of a dark base color with individual white hairs mixed in.
roar: verb. To make a loud, deep cry, especially from pain, anger, or other strong emotion.
rya: noun. A Scandinavian knotted-pile woollen rug, used as bed-cover and tapestry.
yahoo: noun. A rough, coarse, loud or uncouth individual.
yarn: noun. A twisted strand of fibre used for knitting or weaving.
yon: determiner. Distant, but within sight; (that thing) just over there.
aho: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ahom.
aor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aore.
han: verb. plural simple present of have
haro: interjection. An exclamation of distress; alas.
harr: noun. Alternative form of haar (“sea fog; wind which blows in this fog”).
hoa: interjection. ho; hey; a call for attention
hoon: noun. A worthless person; a hooligan or lout.
horry: adjective. impure; unclean; disgustingly dirty; foul
noa: adjective. Non-taboo; serving to replace a taboo name or term.
norry: noun. an improvised rail vehicle from Cambodia; a bamboo train.
noy: verb. To annoy; to harm or injure.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
rna: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Runa.
roary: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a roaring sound.
rohan: noun. An East Indian timber tree.
roon: noun. A shred or remnant (usually of cloth).
yarr: verb. To growl or snarl like a dog.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
